Before learning more, write what you already think.
Answer these questions in your own words:
1.	What is Earth a part of?
2.	What is the Sun?
3.	What is the Moon?
4.	What is a galaxy?
5.	Which is bigger: the Solar System or the Milky Way?

Do not worry if you are not sure. Just write what you know and save your answer. Then tap the robot button and read the AI feedback.
Improve your answer once and tap the robot button again. Read the second feedback, but then move on to the next task.
Your answer does not need to be perfect yet. In this task, we are only finding out what you already know before studying the topic.;;Writing;;None;;;"Open Answer

Watch the video and listen carefully when it explains the idea of a cosmic address.
A cosmic address means where we live in the universe. It starts from a small place, such as a classroom, and then zooms farther and farther out into space.
While watching, try to notice the order:
Earth → Solar System → Milky Way Galaxy → Universe;;Video;;Video Link: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U9TYRSf3xiQ&list=PL41pPir_GAeN5hvEFRbzBD1JN7Zrv6Iio;;;No image

Write your cosmic address

The Planets of the Solar System
The Sun is at the center of our Solar System. It gives off light and heat. Eight planets orbit, or go around, the Sun: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus and Neptune.
The four planets closest to the Sun are Mercury, Venus, Earth and Mars. They are called rocky planets because they have solid surfaces. Earth is one of these planets. It goes around the Sun in about 365 days, which is one year.
Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus and Neptune are farther away from the Sun. They are much bigger than Earth and are made mostly of gases. Saturn is especially famous for its bright rings, but Jupiter, Uranus and Neptune also have rings.
Planets do not make their own light. We can see them because they reflect light from the Sun. Many planets also have moons. A moon is an object that orbits a planet. Earth has one moon, but some planets have many moons.
Pluto used to be called the ninth planet, but today it is classified as a dwarf planet. That means our Solar System has eight planets.;;Passage. Reading;;None;;;No image

column1:
The Sun, planets and moons
The Sun is a star. Powerful reactions inside the Sun release a huge amount of energy as light and heat. Some of this energy reaches Earth and warms our planet.
Planets orbit the Sun. They do not make their own light. We can
see planets in the sky because they reflect light from the Sun.
The Moon orbits Earth. It is the brightest object in the night sky, but it does not make its own light either. The;;Passage : Reading;;None;;;;;;

Moon shines because it reflects sunlight.
Many planets have moons. Earth has one moon, but some planets have many moons. Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus and Neptune also have rings. These rings are made mostly of ice, small rocks and dust.
There are also many smaller objects in our Solar System, such as dwarf planets, rocks, icy chunks and tiny grains of dust.
When a rock from space enters Earth’s atmosphere, it gets very hot and makes a streak of light in the sky. This light is called a shooting star. If the rock hits Earth’s surface, it is called
a meteorite.
A comet is a large icy object in space. When a comet gets close to the Sun, some of its ice turns into gas and dust. Sunlight makes this look like a tail, so a comet is sometimes called a “tailed
star.”;;;;;;;;;;
